zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Elizabeth Branch於1374年出生在英國諾福爾可奧斯比 聖 邁克爾時，她的父親Philip Branch29歲，母親Lady Margery de Mortimer22歲。她於1404年之後與Sir John De Clere在英國英格蘭諾福爾可奧斯比 聖 瑪格麗特結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1441年7月9日在英國英格蘭諾福爾可凱斯托 聖 艾德蒙茲享年67歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾福爾可凱斯托 聖 艾德蒙茲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Elizabeth Branch于1374年出生在英国諾福爾可奧斯比 聖 邁克爾时，她的父亲Philip Branch29岁，母亲Lady Margery de Mortimer22岁。她于1404年之后与Sir John De Clere在英国英格兰諾福爾可奧斯比 聖 瑪格麗特结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1441年7月9日在英国英格兰諾福爾可凱斯托 聖 艾德蒙茲享年67岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾福爾可凱斯托 聖 艾德蒙茲。

nameMeanings.name: Branch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name or nickname from Middle English braunch ‘branch’ (Old French branche, braunche), of uncertain application (compare German Zweig ).
Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames meaning ‘branch’, e.g. German   Zweig   (compare   Labranche  ), French   Branche  , and Swedish   Gren  .

variants.name: Gren
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Gunner, Lars.

Swedish: ornamental name from gren ‘branch’ (Old Norse grein). It is possible that in some cases the name have been chosen with some reference to the notion of the ‘branches’ making up a family ‘tree’. Compare Branch .

variants.name: Zweig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Avrohom, Isadore, Yochanan, Akiva, Arie, Aron, Elchanan, Hyman, Moishe, Moshe, Shimon.  German Gerd, Hans, Manfred, Reinhold, Arnulf, Herta, Kurt.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from Middle High German zwīg ‘branch, bough, twig, graft’, German Zweig, applied perhaps as a nickname for a new member in a family, offspring or as an occupational name for a horticultural expert. The Jewish surname is artificial. Compare Branch .

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: Of uncertain origin. If not simply an invention, it may have been devised as a feminine equivalent of   Alan   (of which it is an anagram), or a shortened form of   Alana .  It seems to have been first used by the American film actress  Lana Turner  (  1920–1995 ), whose original name was  Julia .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Elizabeth Branch was born in 1374, in Ormesby St Michael, Norfolk, England, her father, Philip Branch, was 29 and her mother, Lady Margery de Mortimer, was 22. She married Sir John De Clere after 1404, in Ormesby St Margaret, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 9 July 1441, in Caistor Saint Edmunds, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 67, and was buried in Caistor Saint Edmunds, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé Sir John De Clere après 1404, à Ormesby St Margaret, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 9 juillet 1441, à Caistor Saint Edmunds, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterrée à Caistor Saint Edmunds, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Elizabeth Branch 1374 in Ormesby St Michael, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Philip Branch 29 und ihre Mutter Lady Margery de Mortimer 22. Sie hat nach 1404 in Ormesby St Margaret, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom Sir John De Clere geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 9. Juli 1441 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Caistor Saint Edmunds, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Caistor Saint Edmunds, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Branchが1374年にイギリスノーフォルクオルメスビー スト ミチャエルで生まれた時，父Philip Branchは29歳，母Lady Margery de Mortimerは22歳であった。1404年以後にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクオルメスビー スト マーガレットでSir John De Clereと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1441年7月9日にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクケイストル サイント エドムンズで67歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノーフォルクケイストル サイント エドムンズにて埋葬された。

ko.Caistor Saint Edmunds, Norfolk, England: 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 케이스터 세인트 에드먼드즈
ko.Ormesby St Michael, Norfolk, England, Un: 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 오메스비 스트 믹하얼
ko.Ormsby, Norfolk, England: 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 오메스비 스트 마거릿
ko.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Branch(이)가 1374년 영국 노르폴크 오메스비 스트 믹하얼에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Philip Branch(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Margery de Mortimer(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1404년 이후 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 오메스비 스트 마거릿에서 Sir John De Clere(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1441년 7월 9일 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 케이스터 세인트 에드먼드즈에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 케이스터 세인트 에드먼드즈에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Sir John De Clere após 1404, em Ormesby St Margaret, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 9 de julho de 1441, em Caistor Saint Edmunds, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 67 anos, e foi sepultada em Caistor Saint Edmunds, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Ormsby, Norfolk, England: ормесби ст маргарет, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Elizabeth Branch родилась в 1374 г. в местности ормесби ст мачаел, норфолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Philip Branch было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Margery de Mortimer, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John De Clere после 1404 г. в местности ормесби ст маргарет, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 9 июля 1441 г. в местности цеистор сеинт эдмундс, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 67 лет и была похоронена в местности цеистор сеинт эдмундс, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Elizabeth Branch nació en 1374, en Ormesby St Michael, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Philip Branch, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Lady Margery de Mortimer, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Sir John De Clere después de 1404, en Ormesby St Margaret, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 9 de julio de 1441, en Caistor Saint Edmunds, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 67 años, y fue sepultada en Caistor Saint Edmunds, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
